mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, lowering the equity risk premium and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is continuous and does not require a threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than returns following FEDFUNDS increases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, markets price in a higher probability of recession and demand a larger equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days until the labor deterioration stabilizes.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the average triggered return is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional average (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that reinforces corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures the effect; the interaction of consumption and housing momentum is required because housing leads consumption cycles.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient (RSAFS growth x HOUST growth) is not significant at 10% level in walk-forward regression, or the joint-signal Rank IC is not higher than the maximum of the two individual-series Rank ICs.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in stronger near-term earnings.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 1-month change and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign hit-rate is not above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is falling while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, markets anticipate future rate cuts and price in forward equity gains, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-specific: it requires FEDFUNDS to be above its 12-month average (restrictive stance) combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL, distinguishing it from a stagflation regime where the direction would reverse.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 12-month average AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth decelerating for 3+ consecutive months
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with disinflation (pivot anticipation)
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in the triggered disinflation-pivot regime are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the effect reverses sign when tested on a rolling out-of-sample basis.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating PAYEMS growth and rising UNRATE simultaneously signals a genuine labor-market turning point rather than noise, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. Either series alone is noisy and revision-prone; the joint signal is required to filter false positives from single-month payroll revisions.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to outperform the UNRATE-only signal's Rank IC by at least 0.02, or negative-signal instances show positive average forward returns exceeding the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls below a critical threshold indicating housing starts contraction exceeding 10% from its trailing 12-month peak, it signals an imminent demand-side slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as rate-sensitive sectors reprice quickly.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following triggered HOUST declines are not significantly negative (p > 0.10), or hit-rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS above its 12-month average), a slowdown in INDPRO growth signals that restrictive policy is successfully curbing output, raising recession odds and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. In an easing regime the same INDPRO slowdown is interpreted as already-priced weakness with limited additional negative impact, so the effect is regime-conditional.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 12-month trailing average AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with decelerating industrial output
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in the triggered tightening-slowdown regime are not significantly lower than returns following the same INDPRO slowdown during easing regimes (p > 0.10).
